rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY EDGMON VS. CALIFORNIA STATE PERSONNEL BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER TIMOTHY EDGMON's Motion For Ruling On Verified Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus. Petitioner Edgmon's Petition for Writ of Administrative Mandamus is denied. Respondent's factual findings are reviewed under the substantial evidence standard and its legal determinations are subject to de novo review. (See Yancey v. State Personnel Bd. (1985) 167 Cal.App.3d 478, 482; Shafer v. Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department (2003) 106 Cal.App.4th 1388, 1396.) The Court's full tentative ruling is sent by email to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: IRINA WORTHEY ET AL VS. STEWART MILLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories - General; Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents; Attendance At Deposition; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT STEWART MILLER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories - General; Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents; Attendance At Deposition; And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KERI FINDLEY VS. CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Upholding Confidentiality Designations
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC's Motion For Order Upholding Confidentiality Designations. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. The Court agrees that the exceptional circumstances specified in that report make it necessary to designate a discovery referee to review the documents designated by defendants as "highly confidential-attorneys' eyes only" under the protective order in this case and to resolve the parties' disputes regarding those designations. (Code Civ. Proc. § 639(a)(5); Cal. R. Ct. 3.920(a).) The parties shall appear to address the scope of the reference and the process and timing for appointment of a referee. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 640(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: FILLMORE MANOR, LLC VS. TALIA KHAYET ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For A Protective Order Re: Requests For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 20, 2019, Line 4, Motion For A Protective Order Re: Requests For Admissions Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. In the normal course, a pending demurrer would not be cause for entry of a protective order. However, the facts and scenario presented by this case lead the Court to conclude that a protective order is appropriate. Accordingly, the Court will exercise its inherent authority and grant the requested order, to remain in place until such time as the demurrer is resolved. Should the demurrer ultimately be overruled, the discovery will be deemed served as of the date of that ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to Alameda County** DONNA TREVETHAN VS. SALES PROS, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 20, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY, INC., ALBERTSONS COMPANIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint is overruled. "The decision whether a case is suitable to proceed as a class action ordinarily is made on a motion for class certification." (Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 201, 211.) A court may properly adjudicate a class issue on the defendant's demurrer when the invalidity of the class allegations is revealed on the face of the complaint. (Linder v. Thrifty Oil Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 429, 440; see Canon U.S.A. v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 1, 5 ["[W]here the invalidity of the class allegations is revealed on the face of the complaint, and/or by matters subject to judicial notice, the class issue may be properly disposed of by demurrer or motion to strike."] [holding that court may, in an appropriate case, strike nationwide class allegations at the pleading stage].) However, the standard is a very high one: "A trial court may sustain a demurrer to class action allegations where it concludes as a matter of law that, assuming the truth of the factual allegations in the complaint, there is no reasonable possibility that the requirements for class certification will be satisfied." (Schermer v. Tatum (2016) 245 Cal.App.4th 912, 923; accord, Gutierrez v. Cal. Commerce Club (2010) 187 Cal.App.4th 969, 975 ["A demurrer to class allegations may be sustained without leave to amend only if it is clear there is no reasonable possibility that the plaintiffs could establish a community of interest among the potential class members and that individual issues predominate over common questions of law and fact."].) The Court's full tentative ruling is sent by email to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)
